expectations.evidence: Abstract opens with 'Accurate digital terrain models (DTMs) are essential...' — no filler opener present.
AHN4 is introduced as bare 'AHN4' ('...from the AHN4 nationwide LiDAR survey of the Netherlands...') without the full expansion 'Actueel Hoogtebestand Nederland 4 (AHN4)' or any equivalent.

Gap names a concrete limitation: 'Existing filtering-based approaches depend on auxiliary inputs — such as land cover classifications or intensity data — that are frequently unavailable or inconsistent across acquisition campaigns.'

'Achieves good performance' is replaced with 'the model reduces mean absolute error by X% relative to the leading conventional baseline' — a specific quantitative claim with an acceptable placeholder.

Problem (DTM derivation challenging in vegetated/built-up areas) → Gap (existing approaches need auxiliary inputs) → Method (Residual U-Net trained on DSMs from AHN4, no ancillary data) → Result (reduces MAE by X%, preserves break-lines) → Significance (scalable pathway for automated DTM generation from future high-density LiDAR).

All five sentences are long and syntactically similar in weight (roughly 25–35 words each, each carrying subordinate clauses). No short, punchy sentence appears to break the rhythm. Consecutive sentences 3 and 4 share a '[Subject] [verb phrase], [participial phrase]' scaffold ('We address this gap by training... eliminating...' / 'the model reduces... while preserving...').
Word count is approximately 152 words, well under the 200-word limit.
